steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to explain what makes the neon sculpture glow — that requires a cause or mechanism, not a description of appearance or manufacturing steps.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Benton's 2023 sculpture spells out the phrase "need money for cars" in glowing white neon lights.: describes what the sculpture looks like, not what causes the glow. ✗
  • Choice B — The sculpture's glass tubes have been heated and molded to spell out the phrase "need money for cars.": covers part of the manufacturing process (shaping the tubes), but heating and molding don't make the gas glow. ✗
  • Choice C — A glow emanates from Benton's sculpture, which is made of glass tubes.: restates that the sculpture glows without explaining why. ✗
  • Choice D — An electrical current passes through the gas-filled tubes in Need Money for Cars, making them glow white.: directly states the cause (electrical current through gas) and the effect (glow), matching the note "An electrical current is passed through the gas to make it glow." ✓
That is the correct answer.
